2606.11854.title: Fine-tuning Multi-modal LLMs with ART: Art-based Reinforcement Training
2606.11854.paper_summary: There are two main Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) techniques for Large Language Models (LLMs). While Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) introduces additional weights between the LLM layers, Soft Prompting introduces additional fine-tuning-specific raw tokens to an LLM input. However, both require modification to the computational graphs of precompiled, preoptimized LLMs. As a result, neither is fully supported in high-throughput engines like vLLM. We propose fine-tuning with ART (Art-based Reinforcement Training). The method injects information into a frozen Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) by optimizing only its raw visual input, thus enabling the soft-token approach on pre-compiled computational graphs. It relies on backpropagation of gradients back into a plain pixel array and thus supports any fine-tuning objective. Moreover, the optimized visual input can be stylized as task-relevant computational artworks. The approach's effectiveness is confirmed for different sizes of a popular open Qwen architecture and for several textual benchmarks. Specifically, ART reaches accuracy competitive with LoRA across mathematics and structured-tool-use benchmarks.
2606.11854.paper_summary_zh: 有兩種主要的參數高效微調（PEFT）技術用於大型語言模型（LLMs）。低秩適應（LoRA）在LLM層之間引入額外的權重，而軟提示則在LLM輸入中引入額外的微調專用原始標記。然而，兩者都需要對預編譯、預優化的LLM的計算圖進行修改。因此，這兩者在高吞吐量引擎如vLLM中都不完全受支持。我們提出使用ART（基於藝術的增強訓練）進行微調。該方法通過僅優化其原始視覺輸入，將信息注入到一個凍結的多模態大型語言模型（MLLM）中，從而在預編譯的計算圖上啟用軟標記方法。它依賴於將梯度反向傳播回普通像素數組，因此支持任何微調目標。此外，優化的視覺輸入可以被風格化為與任務相關的計算藝術作品。該方法的有效性在不同大小的流行開放Qwen架構和幾個文本基準上得到了證實。具體而言，ART在數學和結構化工具使用基準上達到了與LoRA相競爭的準確性。
2606.11854.author: Michal Chudoba et.al.
2606.11854.authors: Michal Chudoba, Sergey Alyaev, Petra Galuscakova, Tomasz Wiktorski
